title: Saying Sorry
lesson: When you hurt someone, saying sorry helps make things better.
prompts: Place the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image into a cosy, sunny living room with a soft rug, playing happily with colourful wooden blocks. The person from the input image stands on the far left, facing the camera at eye level with their face fully visible and a cheerful smile. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} kneels beside a tall, neatly stacked block tower, looking proud. Eye-level medium-wide shot, warm afternoon sunlight, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

A scene in the same sunny living room. The {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, mid-step and reaching across, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, with a surprised expression as their arm bumps the tall block tower. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} sits on the rug watching as the wooden blocks tumble and scatter across the floor. Eye-level medium-wide shot, warm light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

The same living room with wooden blocks scattered across the soft rug. The {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left, wringing their hands uncertainly in front of them, facing the camera with their face fully visible and a worried expression. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} sits among the fallen blocks looking sad, with a downturned mouth. Eye-level medium shot, gentle warm light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

The {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands alone on the far left of the cosy living room, facing the camera with their face fully visible, one hand on their chest and a thoughtful, sorry expression as they hesitate. Scattered wooden blocks lie on the rug in the foreground. Eye-level medium shot, soft warm afternoon light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

The {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, kneeling on the rug but with the head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, gently helping to pick up the wooden blocks and rebuild the tower with a kind, apologetic smile. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} kneels too, reaching for a block and beginning to smile again. Eye-level medium-wide shot, warm light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

The same sunny living room with the wooden block tower rebuilt tall behind them. The {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, facing the camera with their face fully visible and a happy, warm smile, sharing a friendly hug. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} hugs back, beaming with joy. Eye-level medium shot, bright cheerful sunlight, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
texts: You were playing blocks with your friend. He had built a tall, wobbly tower. "Look how high!" he said.
As you reached across — crash! Your arm bumped the tower and all the blocks tumbled down.
Your friend looked at the fallen blocks and felt sad. You weren't sure what to do.
You took a deep breath. It was hard to say, but you knew the right thing to do.
"I'm sorry," you said, and you helped build the tower again. Your friend started to smile.
Soon you were laughing and hugging. Saying sorry had made everything better.
